name: cloudquery-plugin-sdk

metadata.name: cloudquery-plugin-sdk
classifiers: License :: OSI Approved :: Apache Software License

documentation:
CloudQuery Plugin SDK for Python
================================================

Overview
-----------

This is the high-level package to use for developing CloudQuery plugins in Python. See [github.com/cloudquery/plugin-sdk-python](https://github.com/cloudquery/plugin-sdk-python) for more information.

methods.name: cloudquery.sdk.scheduler.scheduler.Scheduler.set_post_sync_hook
documentation:
Use this to set a function that will be called after the sync is finished,
a la `defer fn()` in Go (but for a single function, rather than a stack).

This is necessary because plugins use this pattern on their sync method:

```
return self._scheduler.sync(...)
```

So if a plugin has a `state_client`, there's nowhere to call the flush method.

functions.name: cloudquery.sdk.stateclient.stateclient.connected
documentation: Decorator that provides a `backend_plugin` with a gRPC connection to the decorated function.

classes.name: cloudquery.sdk.stateclient.stateclient.StateClientBuilder
documentation:
Provides a `build` method that creates a `ConnectedStateClient` if you pass backend_options,
or `NoOpStateClient` otherwise.

Args:
    backend_options (BackendOptions): which has `connection` & `table_name` strings.

Returns:
    `ConnectedStateClient` or `NoOpStateClient`

classes.name: cloudquery.sdk.stateclient.stateclient.StateClient
documentation:
Abstract class that defines the interface for a state client.

It implements all methods except those that require a connection,
so it is a succinct overview of what a state client does.

classes.name: cloudquery.sdk.stateclient.stateclient.NoOpStateClient
documentation: A state client implementation that does nothing. Used when there is no backend connection.

classes.name: cloudquery.sdk.stateclient.stateclient.ConnectedStateClient
documentation: A state client implementation that connects to a backend plugin via gRPC to read/write state.

classes.name: cloudquery.sdk.stateclient.stateclient.Table
documentation:
Represents a state table with two columns: key and value.
Provides convenience methods for whatever the gRPC requests need.

functions.name: cloudquery.sdk.transformers.transformers.transform_list_of_dict
documentation: Transform json data to arrow record using the given table schema
